articles.title: As Supreme Court weighs Trump's immigration policy, senior citizens join the fight
articles.summary: As the Supreme Court weighs the Trump administration's termination of Temporary Protected Status for Haitians and Syrians, seniors are advocating for protections for their immigrant caregivers.

articles.title: U.S. Gas Prices Climb Further as Effects of Iran War Reverberate
articles.summary: Crude oil prices hit a fresh high since the start of the war in Iran, as energy supplies from the Middle East remain disrupted.

articles.title: In court, Elon Musk accuses OpenAI of trying to 'have your cake and eat it, too'
articles.summary: In his second day on the stand in the trial he launched against OpenAI, Elon Musk said the AI start-up he'd helped found had strayed from its charitable mission.

articles.title: How Trump's EPA head has transformed the agency — and sided with polluters
articles.summary: New Yorker writer Elizabeth Kolbert says EPA chief Lee Zeldin has rescinded regulations, cut or eliminated departments and terminated the jobs of many scientists. Trump calls Zeldin "our secret weapon

articles.title: Lucinda Williams Explains What It Takes to Write a Great Song
articles.summary: The singer-songwriter talks about being self-taught, and reaching down into the deepest, darkest parts of herself to pull out a song.

articles.title: New Details Released About Cole Tomas Allen, Gunman at Correspondents’ Dinner
articles.summary: The details, combined with video, seem to suggest that the gunman was not the person who shot an officer in his protective vest.

articles.title: What the Supreme Court’s Voting Rights Ruling Could Mean for the Midterms
articles.summary: Democrats will lose at least one blue-leaning district in Louisiana, and Florida is drawing a redder map. South Carolina, Tennessee and Missouri could try to draw new maps before voting begins.

articles.title: '8647' got James Comey indicted. What exactly does it mean?
articles.summary: A grand jury charged Comey with threatening Trump's life through his since-deleted 2025 post of seashells forming "8647." Trump is the 47th president, and the term "86" has a few possible meanings.

articles.title: Melania Trump, Queen Camilla and the Look of the Special Relationship
articles.summary: Parsing a state visit told in photo ops, and style.

articles.title: United Arab Emirates announces it's leaving OPEC
articles.summary: The UAE says it will leave OPEC, amid tensions with Saudi Arabia and the chaos of the Iran war.

articles.title: Supreme Court calls Louisiana's House map an 'unconstitutional racial gerrymander'
articles.summary: The court kept Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act intact, but the decision all but guts a landmark law that came out of the Civil Rights Movement and protected the collective voting power of racial mi

articles.title: Greetings from Syria, where a postwar olive harvest offers a long-lost taste of home
articles.summary: In the warm sun, gathering handfuls of hard olives promised a taste of home that residents of a village in the Homs countryside had been missing for nearly 14 years of civil war.

articles.title: The Trump team is quietly eliminating U.S. support for birth control abroad
articles.summary: Congress has allocated more than $500 million for family planning work internationally. The Trump administration hasn't spent it — and the consequences are already being felt.

articles.title: At State Dinner, King Charles Charms the Court of Trump
articles.summary: King Charles III presented President Trump with a golden bell. “Should you ever need to get hold of us,” the king said, “well, just give us a ring!”

articles.title: South Korean court sentences ex-President Yoon to 7 years in prison
articles.summary: An appeals court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest and bypassing a Cabinet meeting before his brief imposition of martial law in December 2024.

articles.title: Extended War Dents Hopes for Housing Market Rebound
articles.summary: Buyers across the country are cautious, while the Miami market seems immune.

articles.title: Could this conference be a 'turning point' for the world's use of fossil fuels?
articles.summary: Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.

articles.title: EU says Meta is failing to keep underage users off Facebook and Instagram
articles.summary: The European Union accused Meta on Wednesday of failing to stop underage users from accessing Facebook and Instagram, in violation of the bloc's digital rules that require sites to protect minors.

articles.title: Palestinian refugee uses art to share the story of his childhood with his daughter
articles.summary: Basel Zaraa was a Palestinian refugee. When his 5-year-old daughter started asking him questions, he told her this story.

articles.title: How the U.K. is reacting to King Charles' visit to the U.S.
articles.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Lord Peter Ricketts, a member of the House of Lords and a former British diplomat, about King Charles' visit to the U.S. during a tumultuous time.

articles.title: Will UAE's departure from OPEC affect the oil cartel's future influence?
articles.summary: What are the impacts of the UAE's decision to pull out of the OPEC oil cartel? NPR's A Martinez speaks with Helima Croft, global head of commodity strategy at RBC Capital Markets.

articles.title: Ukraine accuses Israel of importing grain 'stolen' by Russia
articles.summary: Ukraine accused Israel of allowing the import of grain it claims Russia stole from occupied areas. Israel claimed that the vessel had not entered the port and had not yet submitted its documents.

articles.title: How Taylor Swift Writes a Song, In Her Own Words
articles.summary: The artist shares stories behind some of her biggest hits, her love of a “rant bridge” and how life in the public eye informs the stories she tells in her songs.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
